senses.text: 1580s, from French insister (14c.) or directly from Latin insistere "take a stand, stand on, stand still; follow, pursue; insist, press vigorously, urge, dwell upon," from in- "upon" (see in- (2)) + sistere "take a stand" (see assist (v.)). Perhaps in some cases a back-formation from insistence. Related: Insisted; insisting.
